plant.name: Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company, LLC

background.text:
I.  BACKGROUND
ND  
 
Tesoro has applied for a modification  of the Permit to Operate for the following  
equipment:  
  
S‐854 East Air Flare     S‐944 North Steam Flare 
S‐945 South Steam Flare   S‐992 Emergency  Flare 
S‐1012 West Air Flare     S‐1517 Coker Flare 
S‐1524 50 Unit Flare 
 Tesoro would like to install supplemental  natural gas pipes, valves, controls and related 
monitoring  for these existing flares.  This project is proposed  to meet the Tesoro 
Consent Decree requirements,  which
 replicate the flare control efficiency  requirements  
of the new EPA MACT CC flare standards,  NESHAP 40 CFR 63 Subpart CC (part of the 
recent Refinery Sector Rules ‐‐ aka RSR ‐ amendments),  which will be effective on 
January 30, 2019.  The natural gas will be occasionally  used as supplemental  gas 
necessary  to comply with the 40 CFR 63.670(e),  270 Btu/scf net heating value (NHVcz) 
minimum  limit at combustion  zone, to ensure 98% flare destruction  efficiency  and 
reduced flare emissions.   The NHVcz limit is based on the concept that excess steam in 
the combustion  zone dilutes the combustible  material and decreases  the destruction  
efficiency  of the flare.  Therefore,  the NHVcz limit essentially  requires refineries  to 
optimize their steam to vent gas ratios, and if necessary,  supplement  flare vent gas with 
other gas if the refinery vent gas/steam  combination  cannot meet the minimum  270 
BTU/scf limit alone.  40 CFR 63 Subpart CC does not specify what other gas is to be used 
to meet the minimum  270 BTU/scf limit.  Tesoro currently  uses natural gas for purge 
and pilot gas, and has chosen natural gas, when required,  to meet the 270 BTU/scf limit. 
 Tesoro will install two systems.  One will service the main flare system,  a cascading
 
system of 6 flares on the main flare pad consisting  of S‐854, S‐944, S‐945, S‐992, S‐1012 
and S‐1517.  The second system will service the S‐1524 50 Unit Flare.  The other two 
flares, S‐943 Butane Flare and S‐1013 Ammonia  Plant Flare are not included in this 
permit application.   There are no changes to the flare tips.  Since this permit is necessary  
for modifying  an existing source in order to comply with emission regulations,  this 
project is exempt, per Regulation  1‐115, from new and modified sources requirements  
of Regulations  2‐2‐301 (BACT) or 2‐2‐302 or 2‐2‐303 (offsets). 
 
1-115 Exemption, Modification to Meet Emission Standards: When permits are 
necessary for modifying an existing source in  order to comply with emission regulations 
such modifications shall not subject the existi ng source to emission standards for new or 
modified plants as set forth in Section 2-2- 301 or 2-2-302 or 2-2-303 of Regulation 2, 
Permits.   
Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company, LLC 
Application #29058  Plant # 14628 
Page 2 of 8  6/27/2018

II.  EMISSION  CALCULATIONS
Since the project is exempt from offsets per Regulation  1‐115, the emission calculation  
shown below is for information  only.  The emission increases  will not be added to the 
cumulative  increase. 
 Fugitive Emissions  from New Piping containing  Natural Gas  
Since the emissions  are not part of the facility cumulative  increase, Tesoro was not 
requested  to estimate the number of fugitive components.   However,  based on a permit 
application  from another refinery, the average number of new and replaced fugitive 
components  per supplemental  gas system is about 20 valves and 60 connectors.   
Fugitive emission estimates  are based on compliance  with BAAQMD  Regulation  8‐18.  
 Emissions  are calculated  using the California  Air Pollution Control Officer Association  
(CAPCOA)  Table IV‐3a: CAPCOA Revised 1995 EPA Correlation  Equations,  February 1999, 
and the 100 ppm leak limits for valves (8‐18‐302) and connectors  (8‐18‐
304).  There are 
two supplemental  gas systems proposed  in this permit application.

 waste gas flared at the refinery to 1,090,902  scfd on a 365‐day average.  According

conditions.text:
VIII. CONDITIONS
TIONS  
 
The following  permit conditions  will be imposed for this permit application.   These conditions  
are similar to those imposed on other refinery flare supplemental  gas permit applications,  
edited for Consent Decree requirements.  
 
 
Tesoro Refinery & Marketing Company, LLC 
Plant 14628, Application 29058 Flare Supplemental Gas S-854  East Air Flare S-944  North Steam Flare S-945  South Steam Flare S-992  Emergency Flare S-1012  West Air Flare S-1517  Coker Flare S-1524  50 Unit Flare  1. The owner/operator of Flares S-854, S-944, S-945, S-992, S-1012, S-1517 and S-
1524 shall use only natural gas as a supplemen tal gas necessary to comply with the 
minimum Net Heating Value at combustion zone (NHVcz) of 270 Btu/scf. (Basis: 
NESHAP 40 CFR 63.670(e) - effectiv e January 30, 2019, except S-944 and S-945 
effective October 1, 2017)  
2. The owner/operator of Flares S-854, S-944, S-945, S-992, S-1012, S-1517 and S-
1524 shall have a hydrocarbon destruction effi ciency of at least 98 wt.% POC on a 
mass basis.  (Basis: Re gulations 2-1-403, 12-11-401.9) 
 
3. The owner/operator of Flares S- 854, S-944, S-945, S-992, S-1012 and S-1517 shall 
limit the total Main Flare Sy stem use of natural gas as  supplemental flare gas to 
14,000 therms/hr and 1,700,000 therms in any consecutive 12-month period. (Basis: 
Toxics, Regulation 2-1-320, 2-1-403) 
Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company, LLC 
Application #29058  Plant # 14628 
Page 7 of 8  6/27/2018  
4. The owner/operator of S-1524 shall limit the total 50 Unit Flare System use of natural 
gas as supplemental flare gas to 14,0 00 therms/hr and 1,700,000 therms in any 
consecutive 12-month period. (Basis: Toxics, Regulation 2-1-320, 2-1-403) 
 
5. To demonstrate compliance with Parts 3 and 4 of this permit condition, the 
owner/operator shall install a gas flow ra te monitor to measure the supplemental 
natural gas usage. (Basis: NESHAP 40 CFR 63.670(i))  
6. The owner/operator of Flares S-854, S-944, S-945, S-992, S-1012, S-1517 and S-
1524 shall update and maintain the Flare Minimization Plan (FMP) as required by 
Regulation 12-12-404. (Basis: Regulation 12, Rule 12)  
7. The owner/operator of Flares S-854, S-944, S-945, S-992, S-1012, S-1517 and S-
1524 shall install and operate a continuous parametric monitoring system (CPMS) 
along with a CPMS monitoring plan as re quired by and consistent with 40 CFR 
63.671(b).  (Basis: NESHAP 40 CFR 63.671, Regulation 1-523)  
8. The owner/operator shall maintain all r ecords and reports required by this permit 
condition in a District-appr oved log. The following records shall be kept on site and 
shall be made available for District inspect ion for a period of at least 5 years from the 
date on which a record is made. (Bas is: Recordkeeping, NESHAP 40 CFR 63.670(e)) 
a.  Total daily flow rate of natural gas as supplemental gas to the flare, summarized 
on a consecutive 12-month period basis b.  Daily net heating value of the flare vent gas (NHVvg) and calculation of net 
heating value in the combustion zone (NHVcz) 
c.  Daily flare steam to vent gas ratio  
 
Tesoro reviewed  these conditions  and had two comments.  
 Delete Part 2 because there is currently  no method for determining  the destruction  
efficiency  for flares.  The District does not agree with the proposed  deletion.  Firstly, there 
are other similar flare requirements  currently  applicable  (e.g., 24323‐7) so this Part 2 is 
not new.  Secondly,  compliance  is demonstrated  by meeting the requirements  of the 
consent decree and 40 CFR 63.670 (the fundamental  basis of these RSR requirements  is to 
assure 98% destruction  efficiency).  
 Delete the recordkeeping  requirements  for steam because there is no such requirement  
in the consent decree or the new RSR (40 CFR 63.670).  A similar comment  was made 
regarding  this same permit conditions  requirement  at another refinery.  Consistent  with 
the other refinery permit application,  the District does not agree with the proposed  
deletion.  In the evaluation  of this other refinery supplemental  gas permit application,  
District staff contacted  EPA's RSR staff.  EPA advised that one of the primary findings that 
led to the flare RSR amendments  was that many operators  over‐steamed the flares to 
minimize  smoke.  This over‐steaming,  at least in some cases, was at the expense of 
combustion  efficiency.   The primary intent of the 63.670(e)  combustion  zone heat content 
limit was to optimize the flare steaming.   Then, once steam rate is reduced to optimum

EVALUATION  REPORT 
Tesoro Refining and Marketing  Company,  LLC 
Application  #29058‐ Plant # 14628 
150 Solano Way 
Martinez,  CA  94553 
 
I.  BACKGROUND  
 
Tesoro has applied for a modification  of the Permit to Operate for the following  
equipment:  
  
S‐854 East Air Flare     S‐944 North Steam Flare 
S‐945 South Steam Flare   S‐992 Emergency  Flare 
S‐1012 West Air Flare     S‐1517 Coker Flare 
S‐1524 50 Unit Flare 
 Tesoro would like to install supplemental  natural gas pipes, valves, controls and related 
monitoring  for these existing flares.  This project is proposed  to meet the Tesoro 
Consent Decree requirements,  which
 replicate the flare control efficiency  requirements  
of the new EPA MACT CC flare standards,  NESHAP 40 CFR 63 Subpart CC (part of the 
recent Refinery Sector Rules ‐‐ aka RSR ‐ amendments),  which will be effective on 
January 30, 2019.  The natural gas will be occasionally  used as supplemental  gas 
necessary  to comply with the 40 CFR 63.670(e),  270 Btu/scf net heating value (NHVcz) 
minimum  limit at combustion  zone, to ensure 98% flare destruction  efficiency  and 
reduced flare emissions.   The NHVcz limit is based on the concept that excess steam in 
the combustion  zone dilutes the combustible  material and decreases  the destruction  
efficiency  of the flare.  Therefore,  the NHVcz limit essentially  requires refineries  to 
optimize their steam to vent gas ratios, and if necessary,  supplement  flare vent gas with 
other gas if the refinery vent gas/steam  combination  cannot meet the minimum  270 
BTU/scf limit alone.  40 CFR 63 Subpart CC does not specify what other gas is to be used 
to meet the minimum  270 BTU/scf limit.  Tesoro currently  uses natural gas for purge 
and pilot gas, and has chosen natural gas, when required,  to meet the 270 BTU/scf limit. 
 Tesoro will install two systems.  One will service the main flare system,  a cascading
 
system of 6 flares on the main flare pad consisting  of S‐854, S‐944, S‐945, S‐992, S‐1012 
and S‐1517.  The second system will service the S‐1524 50 Unit Flare.  The other two 
flares, S‐943 Butane Flare and S‐1013 Ammonia  Plant Flare are not included in this 
permit application.   There are no changes to the flare tips.  Since this permit is necessary  
for modifying  an existing source in order to comply with emission regulations,  this 
project is exempt, per Regulation  1‐115, from new and modified sources requirements  
of Regulations  2‐2‐301 (BACT) or 2‐2‐302 or 2‐2‐303 (offsets). 
 
1-115 Exemption, Modification to Meet Emission Standards: When permits are 
necessary for modifying an existing source in  order to comply with emission regulations 
such modifications shall not subject the existi ng source to emission standards for new or 
modified plants as set forth in Section 2-2- 301 or 2-2-302 or 2-2-303 of Regulation 2, 
Permits.

II.  EMISSION  CALCULATIONS  
 
Since the project is exempt from offsets per Regulation  1‐115, the emission calculation  
shown below is for information  only.  The emission increases  will not be added to the 
cumulative  increase. 
 Fugitive Emissions  from New Piping containing  Natural Gas  
Since the emissions  are not part of the facility cumulative  increase, Tesoro was not 
requested  to estimate the number of fugitive components.   However,  based on a permit 
application  from another refinery, the average number of new and replaced fugitive 
components  per supplemental  gas system is about 20 valves and 60 connectors.   
Fugitive emission estimates  are based on compliance  with BAAQMD  Regulation  8‐18.

(CAPCOA)  Table IV‐3a: CAPCOA Revised 1995 EPA Correlation  Equations,  February 1999, 
and the 100 ppm leak limits for valves (8‐18‐302) and connectors  (8‐18‐
304).  There are 
two supplemental  gas systems proposed  in this permit application.

3. The owner/operator of Flares S- 854, S-944, S-945, S-992, S-1012 and S-1517 shall

8. The owner/operator shall maintain all r ecords and reports required by this permit

a.  Total daily flow rate of natural gas as supplemental gas to the flare, summarized 
on a consecutive 12-month period basis b.  Daily net heating value of the flare vent gas (NHVvg) and calculation of net 
heating value in the combustion zone (NHVcz) 
c.  Daily flare steam to vent gas ratio  
 
Tesoro reviewed  these conditions  and had two comments.  
 Delete Part 2 because there is currently  no method for determining  the destruction  
efficiency  for flares.  The District does not agree with the proposed  deletion.  Firstly, there 
are other similar flare requirements  currently  applicable  (e.g., 24323‐7) so this Part 2 is 
not new.  Secondly,  compliance  is demonstrated  by meeting the requirements  of the 
consent decree and 40 CFR 63.670 (the fundamental  basis of these RSR requirements  is to 
assure 98% destruction  efficiency).  
 Delete the recordkeeping  requirements  for steam because there is no such requirement  
in the consent decree or the new RSR (40 CFR 63.670).  A similar comment  was made 
regarding  this same permit conditions  requirement  at another refinery.  Consistent  with 
the other refinery permit application,  the District does not agree with the proposed  
deletion.  In the evaluation  of this other refinery supplemental  gas permit application,  
District staff contacted  EPA's RSR staff.  EPA advised that one of the primary findings that 
led to the flare RSR amendments  was that many operators  over‐steamed the flares to 
minimize  smoke.  This over‐steaming,  at least in some cases, was at the expense of 
combustion  efficiency.   The primary intent of the 63.670(e)  combustion  zone heat content 
limit was to optimize the flare steaming.   Then, once steam rate is reduced to optimum  
Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company, LLC 
Application #29058  Plant # 14628 
Page 8 of 8  6/27/2018 levels, if the combustion  zone heat content is still not above 270 Btu/scf, then a 
supplemental  gas can be added.  Tesoro is correct that there are no steam recordkeeping  
requirements  in the consent decree or the RSR rules.  However,  the District requires this 
information,  consistent  with other refinery permit conditions,  in order to determine  if the 
steam rate has been optimized  and that there is no excess supplemental  gas combustion.

blocks.heading: I.  BACKGROUND
blocks.text:
I.  BACKGROUND
ND  
 
Tesoro has applied for a modification  of the Permit to Operate for the following  
equipment:  
  
S‐854 East Air Flare     S‐944 North Steam Flare 
S‐945 South Steam Flare   S‐992 Emergency  Flare 
S‐1012 West Air Flare     S‐1517 Coker Flare 
S‐1524 50 Unit Flare 
 Tesoro would like to install supplemental  natural gas pipes, valves, controls and related 
monitoring  for these existing flares.  This project is proposed  to meet the Tesoro 
Consent Decree requirements,  which
 replicate the flare control efficiency  requirements  
of the new EPA MACT CC flare standards,  NESHAP 40 CFR 63 Subpart CC (part of the 
recent Refinery Sector Rules ‐‐ aka RSR ‐ amendments),  which will be effective on 
January 30, 2019.  The natural gas will be occasionally  used as supplemental  gas 
necessary  to comply with the 40 CFR 63.670(e),  270 Btu/scf net heating value (NHVcz) 
minimum  limit at combustion  zone, to ensure 98% flare destruction  efficiency  and 
reduced flare emissions.   The NHVcz limit is based on the concept that excess steam in 
the combustion  zone dilutes the combustible  material and decreases  the destruction  
efficiency  of the flare.  Therefore,  the NHVcz limit essentially  requires refineries  to 
optimize their steam to vent gas ratios, and if necessary,  supplement  flare vent gas with 
other gas if the refinery vent gas/steam  combination  cannot meet the minimum  270 
BTU/scf limit alone.  40 CFR 63 Subpart CC does not specify what other gas is to be used 
to meet the minimum  270 BTU/scf limit.  Tesoro currently  uses natural gas for purge 
and pilot gas, and has chosen natural gas, when required,  to meet the 270 BTU/scf limit. 
 Tesoro will install two systems.  One will service the main flare system,  a cascading
 
system of 6 flares on the main flare pad consisting  of S‐854, S‐944, S‐945, S‐992, S‐1012 
and S‐1517.  The second system will service the S‐1524 50 Unit Flare.  The other two 
flares, S‐943 Butane Flare and S‐1013 Ammonia  Plant Flare are not included in this 
permit application.   There are no changes to the flare tips.  Since this permit is necessary  
for modifying  an existing source in order to comply with emission regulations,  this 
project is exempt, per Regulation  1‐115, from new and modified sources requirements  
of Regulations  2‐2‐301 (BACT) or 2‐2‐302 or 2‐2‐303 (offsets). 
 
1-115 Exemption, Modification to Meet Emission Standards: When permits are 
necessary for modifying an existing source in  order to comply with emission regulations 
such modifications shall not subject the existi ng source to emission standards for new or 
modified plants as set forth in Section 2-2- 301 or 2-2-302 or 2-2-303 of Regulation 2, 
Permits.

‐  All flares are subject to and expected  to be in compliance  with Regulation  1‐
Parametric  Monitor.  Tesoro will install a flow meter and associated  temperature  
and pressure monitors  on each of the new natural gas lines.  These meters will be 
subject to Regulation  1‐523, Parametric  monitoring  requirements.  
 
‐  All flares are subject to Regulation  8, Rule 18‐ Equipment  Leaks.  The equipment  
should comply with the Standards  of Regulation  8, Rule 18 for Valves, Compressors  
and Flanges.  The VOC leak standards  for valves, pumps and flanges are 100 ppmvd.

blocks.heading: VIII. CONDITIONS
blocks.text:
VIII. CONDITIONS
TIONS  
 
The following  permit conditions  will be imposed for this permit application.   These conditions  
are similar to those imposed on other refinery flare supplemental  gas permit applications,  
edited for Consent Decree requirements.  
 
 
Tesoro Refinery & Marketing Company, LLC 
Plant 14628, Application 29058 Flare Supplemental Gas S-854  East Air Flare S-944  North Steam Flare S-945  South Steam Flare S-992  Emergency Flare S-1012  West Air Flare S-1517  Coker Flare S-1524  50 Unit Flare  1. The owner/operator of Flares S-854, S-944, S-945, S-992, S-1012, S-1517 and S-
1524 shall use only natural gas as a supplemen tal gas necessary to comply with the 
minimum Net Heating Value at combustion zone (NHVcz) of 270 Btu/scf. (Basis: 
NESHAP 40 CFR 63.670(e) - effectiv e January 30, 2019, except S-944 and S-945 
effective October 1, 2017)  
2. The owner/operator of Flares S-854, S-944, S-945, S-992, S-1012, S-1517 and S-
1524 shall have a hydrocarbon destruction effi ciency of at least 98 wt.% POC on a 
mass basis.  (Basis: Re gulations 2-1-403, 12-11-401.9) 
 
3. The owner/operator of Flares S- 854, S-944, S-945, S-992, S-1012 and S-1517 shall 
limit the total Main Flare Sy stem use of natural gas as  supplemental flare gas to 
14,000 therms/hr and 1,700,000 therms in any consecutive 12-month period. (Basis: 
Toxics, Regulation 2-1-320, 2-1-403) 
Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company, LLC 
Application #29058  Plant # 14628 
Page 7 of 8  6/27/2018  
4. The owner/operator of S-1524 shall limit the total 50 Unit Flare System use of natural 
gas as supplemental flare gas to 14,0 00 therms/hr and 1,700,000 therms in any 
consecutive 12-month period. (Basis: Toxics, Regulation 2-1-320, 2-1-403) 
 
5. To demonstrate compliance with Parts 3 and 4 of this permit condition, the 
owner/operator shall install a gas flow ra te monitor to measure the supplemental 
natural gas usage. (Basis: NESHAP 40 CFR 63.670(i))  
6. The owner/operator of Flares S-854, S-944, S-945, S-992, S-1012, S-1517 and S-
1524 shall update and maintain the Flare Minimization Plan (FMP) as required by 
Regulation 12-12-404. (Basis: Regulation 12, Rule 12)  
7. The owner/operator of Flares S-854, S-944, S-945, S-992, S-1012, S-1517 and S-
1524 shall install and operate a continuous parametric monitoring system (CPMS) 
along with a CPMS monitoring plan as re quired by and consistent with 40 CFR 
63.671(b).  (Basis: NESHAP 40 CFR 63.671, Regulation 1-523)  
8. The owner/operator shall maintain all r ecords and reports required by this permit 
condition in a District-appr oved log. The following records shall be kept on site and 
shall be made available for District inspect ion for a period of at least 5 years from the 
date on which a record is made. (Bas is: Recordkeeping, NESHAP 40 CFR 63.670(e)) 
a.  Total daily flow rate of natural gas as supplemental gas to the flare, summarized 
on a consecutive 12-month period basis b.  Daily net heating value of the flare vent gas (NHVvg) and calculation of net 
heating value in the combustion zone (NHVcz) 
c.  Daily flare steam to vent gas ratio  
 
Tesoro reviewed  these conditions  and had two comments.  
 Delete Part 2 because there is currently  no method for determining  the destruction  
efficiency  for flares.  The District does not agree with the proposed  deletion.  Firstly, there 
are other similar flare requirements  currently  applicable  (e.g., 24323‐7) so this Part 2 is 
not new.  Secondly,  compliance  is demonstrated  by meeting the requirements  of the 
consent decree and 40 CFR 63.670 (the fundamental  basis of these RSR requirements  is to 
assure 98% destruction  efficiency).  
 Delete the recordkeeping  requirements  for steam because there is no such requirement  
in the consent decree or the new RSR (40 CFR 63.670).  A similar comment  was made 
regarding  this same permit conditions  requirement  at another refinery.  Consistent  with 
the other refinery permit application,  the District does not agree with the proposed  
deletion.  In the evaluation  of this other refinery supplemental  gas permit application,  
District staff contacted  EPA's RSR staff.  EPA advised that one of the primary findings that 
led to the flare RSR amendments  was that many operators  over‐steamed the flares to 
minimize  smoke.  This over‐steaming,  at least in some cases, was at the expense of 
combustion  efficiency.   The primary intent of the 63.670(e)  combustion  zone heat content 
limit was to optimize the flare steaming.   Then, once steam rate is reduced to optimum  
Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company, LLC 
Application #29058  Plant # 14628 
Page 8 of 8  6/27/2018 levels, if the combustion  zone heat content is still not above 270 Btu/scf, then a 
supplemental  gas can be added.  Tesoro is correct that there are no steam recordkeeping  
requirements  in the consent decree or the RSR rules.  However,  the District requires this 
information,  consistent  with other refinery permit conditions,  in order to determine  if the 
steam rate has been optimized  and that there is no excess supplemental  gas combustion.

unmapped_blocks.text:
V.  BEST AVAILABLE  CONTROL  TECHNOLOGY
GY  
   BACT does not apply to all sources mentioned  above per Regulation  1‐115, Exemption.   
However,  all flares meet BACT guideline  with control efficiency  greater than or equal to 
98%, which EPA ensured by its NHVcz standard of minimum  270 Btu/scf.
